channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: U.S. defeats Australia in a key World Cup game
item.description: The United States defeated Australia in an important group match at the World Cup. The 2-0 victory means the U.S. will advance to the tournament's knockout round.
item.content:encoded: The United States defeated Australia in an important group match at the World Cup. The 2-0 victory means the U.S. will advance to the tournament's knockout round.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Space news: Tiny moon explorers, a debate about the expanding universe, and more.
item.description: In this space news roundup, Berly McCoy and Regina Barber of NPR's Short Wave talk about tiny autonomous "transformers" that can explore the moon, a debate about the expanding universe, and more.
item.content:encoded: In this space news roundup, Berly McCoy and Regina Barber of NPR's Short Wave talk about tiny autonomous "transformers" that can explore the moon, a debate about the expanding universe, and more.
item.dc:creator: Regina G. Barber

item.title: 'Grandmother of Juneteenth' celebrates freedom, 2.5 miles at a time
item.description: Opal Lee is known as the Grandmother of Juneteenth. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talked with her in 2024 about her activism, and her hopes for the future.
item.content:encoded: Opal Lee is known as the Grandmother of Juneteenth. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talked with her in 2024 about her activism, and her hopes for the future.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan

item.title: The view from Beirut hours into latest ceasefire
item.description: Just hours into the ceasefire, we check on Lebanon to see if this delicate agreement is actually holding.
item.content:encoded: Just hours into the ceasefire, we check on Lebanon to see if this delicate agreement is actually holding.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Hodges

item.title: Scottish 'Tartan Army' drinks Boston dry
item.description: Scottish soccer fans have taken over Boston to watch the World Cup. The Tartan Army, as they are called, brought their Scottish traditions with them: Kilts, bagpipes and lots of drinking.
item.content:encoded: Scottish soccer fans have taken over Boston to watch the World Cup. The Tartan Army, as they are called, brought their Scottish traditions with them: Kilts, bagpipes and lots of drinking.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Marquez Janse

item.title: Chicago celebrates Obama Presidential Center opening, but community concerns persist
item.description: Some supporters view the Obama Presidential Center as a welcomed investment in a historically underserved area, while long-time residents and community members worry it may accelerate gentrification.
item.content:encoded: Some supporters view the Obama Presidential Center as a welcomed investment in a historically underserved area, while long-time residents and community members worry it may accelerate gentrification.
item.dc:creator: Araceli Gomez-Aldana

item.title: In a New York district, a fight for the future of the left
item.description: Rep. Nydia Velázquez's retirement has opened up a competitive primary in a New York City Congressional district. The race to replace her is a proxy battle about the future of the Democratic Party.
item.content:encoded: Rep. Nydia Velázquez's retirement has opened up a competitive primary in a New York City Congressional district. The race to replace her is a proxy battle about the future of the Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: Cape Verde's goalie, the overnight sensation
item.description: On any given week, there could be a main character that the internet is obsessed with. Right now, it's the Cape Verde team's goal keeper – Josimar Jose Evora Dias – better known as Vozinha.
item.content:encoded: On any given week, there could be a main character that the internet is obsessed with. Right now, it's the Cape Verde team's goal keeper – Josimar Jose Evora Dias – better known as Vozinha.
item.dc:creator: Mia Venkat

item.title: In his latest album, Baauer wants to bring 'U' joy
item.description: With his latest album U, Baauer aims to inject good vibes into the world. It's a sunny collage of disco, house, breakbeats and more, influenced by the sounds of 2000s dance music.
item.content:encoded: With his latest album U, Baauer aims to inject good vibes into the world. It's a sunny collage of disco, house, breakbeats and more, influenced by the sounds of 2000s dance music.
item.dc:creator: Kai McNamee

item.title: A new study rewrites the history of the plague
item.description: A new study looks at one of the most prolific pathogens in human history — the plague.
item.content:encoded: A new study looks at one of the most prolific pathogens in human history — the plague.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott
